edges.description: Continued growth can produce abdominal symptoms, biliary obstruction, and metastatic spread, especially to the liver.
edges.source: Silent Well-Differentiated Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor Growth

edges.description: Chromatin-regulator inactivation contributes to neuroendocrine tumor formation and progression.
edges.target: Silent Well-Differentiated Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor Growth

edges.description: mTOR pathway dysregulation can contribute to neuroendocrine tumor growth and progression.
edges.target: Silent Well-Differentiated Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor Growth

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: DAXX and ATRX were mutated in 17 and 12 PanNETs, respectively.

nodes.description: Everolimus targets mTOR signaling and is used for progressive advanced pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Everolimus for advanced pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: Everolimus, as compared with placebo, significantly prolonged progression-free survival among patients with progressive advanced pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors and was associated with a low rate of severe adverse events.

meta.association: Recurrent somatic driver or inherited predisposition gene

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: 44% of the tumors had somatic inactivating mutations in MEN1, which encodes menin, a component of a histone methyltransferase complex

nodes.description: Recurrent PanNET alterations include MEN1 and DAXX/ATRX chromatin-regulator inactivation, supporting disordered chromatin state and tumor suppressor loss in well-differentiated pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor cells.

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: The most frequently mutated genes specify proteins implicated in chromatin remodeling: 44% of the tumors had somatic inactivating mutations in MEN1, which encodes menin, a component of a histone methyltransferase complex,

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: DAXX and ATRX were mutated in 17 and 12 PanNETs, respectively.

nodes.description: Lack of an early hormone syndrome can delay recognition until tumor bulk, gastrointestinal symptoms, biliary obstruction, or metastasis is present.

evidence.reference_title: Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumors-A Descriptive Study of the Presenting Features in a 20-Year Surgical Resection Cohort at a Tertiary Institution.
evidence.snippet: Nonfunctional tumors were much more prevalent (77.5%), with functional tumors primarily being insulinomas (75.9%).

evidence.reference_title: Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumors-A Descriptive Study of the Presenting Features in a 20-Year Surgical Resection Cohort at a Tertiary Institution.
evidence.snippet: Metastasis was observed in 39.4% of patients at the time of diagnosis, predominantly in the liver.

nodes.description: Neoplastic pancreatic neuroendocrine cells proliferate as a well-differentiated tumor in the pancreas. Because the tumor is non-functional, early disease may not cause a hormone hypersecretion syndrome and can remain clinically silent.
nodes.id: Silent Well-Differentiated Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor Growth

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: Nonfunctional PanNETs grow silently and patients often present with either an asymptomatic abdominal mass or symptoms of abdominal pain secondary to compression by a large tumor.

evidence.reference_title: The Silent Presentation of a Non-functioning Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Non-functioning PNETs are often asymptomatic and detected incidentally.

nodes.description: A subset of PanNETs has recurrent alterations in mTOR pathway components, including TSC2, supporting dysregulated TORC1 signaling and a therapeutic rationale for mTOR inhibition.

evidence.reference_title: DAXX/ATRX, MEN1, and mTOR pathway genes are frequently altered in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
evidence.snippet: We also found mutations in genes in the mTOR (mammalian target of rapamycin) pathway in 14% of the tumors, a finding that could potentially be used to stratify patients for treatment with mTOR inhibitors.
